content:
💡 vt-cli | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Interacting with the VirusTotal API and its threat intelligence data via a command-line interface.
✨ Key Features: • Retrieve information about files, URLs, domains, IP addresses • Search for files and URLs using VirusTotal Intelligence query syntax • Download files • Manage LiveHunt YARA rules • Launch and manage Retrohunt jobs
📖 Summary: The VirusTotal CLI is a command-line interface tool that allows users to interact with the VirusTotal API. It enables users to retrieve information about files, URLs, domains, and IP addresses, search for threats, download files, and manage YARA rules and Retrohunt jobs directly from their terminal.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ LinuxPlay | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Providing an ultra-low latency remote desktop experience for Linux, particularly for gaming and power users who require fine-grained control over the streaming process.
✨ Key Features: • Ultra-low latency remote desktop and game streaming • Support for H.264 and H.265 (HEVC) codecs • Hardware acceleration (NVENC, QSV, VAAPI, AMF)
📖 Summary: LinuxPlay is an experimental, open-source remote desktop and game streaming solution for Linux hosts and Windows clients. It prioritizes ultra-low latency and granular control over codecs, transport, and capture methods, leveraging FFmpeg and UDP for efficient streaming.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 GNews | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Programmatically accessing and retrieving news articles from Google News for various applications and analyses.
✨ Key Features: • Search Google News articles via API • Return results in JSON format • Fetch top news • Search by keywords, major topics, geo-location, and site • Retrieve full article content
📖 Summary: GNews is a lightweight Python package that provides an API to search for articles on Google News. It allows users to fetch top news, news by keywords, major topics, geo-location, or specific sites, and can also retrieve the full article content.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ b4 | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Circumventing network censorship and restrictions imposed by Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) systems.
✨ Key Features: • Bypasses Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) • Uses netfilter queue manipulation • Provides a web UI for management • Real-time metrics (connections, packets, bandwidth) • Logs streaming with filtering
📖 Summary: B4 is a network packet processor designed to bypass Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) systems. It achieves this by manipulating network traffic using netfilter queueing on Linux systems, offering a user-friendly web interface for management and real-time monitoring.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Canban | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A personal productivity tool for managing tasks and projects locally within a web browser, prioritizing speed, privacy, and a modern user interface.
✨ Key Features: • Local-First Architecture (Zero backend, offline capability, data privacy) • Glassmorphism UI (Sleek, translucent, blurred, layered design) • Hardware Accelerated Drag & Drop • Advanced Theming Engine (Automatic Light/Dark mode detection) • Internationalization (i18n) for multiple languages
📖 Summary: Canban is a hyper-optimized, visually stunning, and privacy-focused local Kanban board application. It runs entirely in the browser with no backend or database, storing data locally using `localStorage` and featuring a premium Glassmorphism UI with advanced theming and internationalization.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
